name: blog-constructor
description: Ready-made blog CMS toolkit providing feed headers, post cards, post metadata, pagination, search, and layout wrappers for building blog listing and detail pages.
when_to_use: Ready-made blog CMS toolkit providing feed headers, post cards, post metadata, pagination, search, and layout wrappers for building blog listing and detail pages
avoid: Not a general content framework — purpose-built for blog/article list and post pages. Not for application UI or landing pages — use uikit or page-constructor respectively.
not_for: Not a general content framework — purpose-built for blog/article list and post pages. Not for application UI or landing pages — use uikit or page-constructor respectively.

name: BlogConstructorProvider
description: The root context provider for the blog-constructor library. Wraps children with all necessary contexts: theme, locale, router, mobile state, device info, analytics, and settings. Must be rendered at the top of every blog-constructor component tree.
when_to_use: Wrap the root of your blog application to provide all blog-constructor contexts
Required before using any other blog-constructor component
avoid: Do not nest multiple BlogConstructorProvider instances — use one at the application root

props.name: isMobile
props.description: Whether the app is rendering in mobile context

props.name: router
props.description: Router context for link handling (defaults to empty object)

props.name: device
props.description: Device context properties (defaults to empty object)

props.name: analytics
props.description: Analytics context for event tracking (defaults to empty object)

props.name: settings
props.description: Settings context overrides (defaults to empty object)

props.name: children
props.description: Child components to wrap with all contexts

examples:
<BlogConstructorProvider
  locale={{ lang: 'en' }}
  theme="light"
  router={{ pathname: '/blog', query: {} }}
  analytics={{ sendEvents: trackEvent }}
>
  <BlogPage ... />
</BlogConstructorProvider>

name: BlogPage
description: A full blog feed page container that composes PageConstructor with blog-specific contexts (feeds, likes, authentication). Renders the page content blocks via page-constructor and overlays PromptSignIn when unauthenticated users try to like a post.
when_to_use: As the top-level component for a blog listing/feed page
When integrating blog-constructor with a page-constructor page content definition
avoid: For individual post detail pages — use BlogPostPage instead
Without wrapping in BlogConstructorProvider

props.name: content
props.description: page-constructor PageContent definition for the blog feed page

props.name: posts
props.description: Posts data including posts array, optional pinnedPost, and total count

props.name: getPosts
props.description: Function to fetch posts with updated query parameters

props.name: hasLikes
props.description: Whether like functionality is enabled (default false)

props.name: metaData
props.description: SEO meta data for the page

props.name: custom
props.description: Custom component map extensions for page-constructor

props.name: pageCountForShowSupportButtons
props.description: Passed to Paginator for prev/next button visibility

props.name: isSignedInUser
props.description: Whether the current user is signed in (default false)

props.name: onClickSignIn
props.description: Sign-in handler for unauthenticated like attempts

examples:
<BlogConstructorProvider locale={{ lang: 'en' }}>
  <BlogPage
    content={pageContent}
    posts={{ posts, pinnedPost, count: totalCount }}
    tags={tags}
    getPosts={fetchPosts}
    hasLikes
    toggleLike={handleToggleLike}
    isSignedInUser={isLoggedIn}
    onClickSignIn={() => router.push('/signin')}
  />
</BlogConstructorProvider>

name: BlogPostPage
description: A full blog post detail page container that composes PageConstructor with post-specific contexts (post data, likes, suggested posts, sharing). Renders the post page content blocks and overlays PromptSignIn when unauthenticated users try to like.
when_to_use: As the top-level component for a blog post detail/article page
When integrating blog-constructor with a page-constructor PageContent definition for individual posts
avoid: For blog feed/listing pages — use BlogPage instead
Without wrapping in BlogConstructorProvider

props.name: content
props.description: page-constructor PageContent definition for the post page

props.name: suggestedPosts
props.description: Array of suggested/related posts to display

props.name: metaData
props.description: SEO meta data for the post page

props.name: likes
props.type: { hasUserLike?: boolean; likesCount?: number; toggleLike?: ToggleLikeCallbackType }
props.description: Like state and toggle callback; omit to hide like functionality

props.name: custom
props.description: Custom component map extensions for page-constructor

props.name: isSignedInUser
props.description: Whether the current user is signed in (default false)

props.name: onClickSignIn
props.description: Sign-in handler for unauthenticated like attempts

props.name: breadcrumbs
props.description: Breadcrumb navigation props for the post header

examples:
<BlogConstructorProvider locale={{ lang: 'en' }}>
  <BlogPostPage
    content={postPageContent}
    post={post}
    suggestedPosts={relatedPosts}
    likes={{
      hasUserLike: userLiked,
      likesCount: post.likesCount,
      toggleLike: handleToggleLike,
    }}
    isSignedInUser={isLoggedIn}
    onClickSignIn={() => router.push('/signin')}
    breadcrumbs={{ items: [{ text: 'Blog', href: '/blog' }] }}
  />
</BlogConstructorProvider>

name: FeedHeader
description: Renders the header section of a blog feed page, including background image, theme, and filter controls (tags, services, search). Integrates with page-constructor's Grid and BackgroundImage for layout.
when_to_use: At the top of a blog listing or feed page
When users need to filter blog posts by tag or service
When a themed header with optional background image is required
avoid: On individual blog post pages — use PostInfo instead
When no filtering controls are needed — use a plain heading
examples:
<FeedHeader
  title="Blog"
  tags={[
    { id: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
    { id: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
    { id: 'devops', name: 'DevOps' },
  ]}
  queryParams={{ tag: 'frontend', page: 1 }}
  handleLoadData={(params) => router.push({ query: params })}
/>

<FeedHeader
  title="Engineering Blog"
  theme="dark"
  verticalOffset="l"
  background={{
    light: { url: '/images/blog-header-bg.jpg' },
    dark: { url: '/images/blog-header-bg-dark.jpg' },
  }}
  services={[
    { id: 'cloud', name: 'Cloud' },
    { id: 'platform', name: 'Platform' },
  ]}
  handleLoadData={(params) => fetchPosts(params)}
  queryParams={{}}
/>

name: MetaWrapper
description: Conditionally wraps meta content in a react-helmet Helmet component for SSR head tag injection. When needHelmetWrapper is false, renders the metaComponent directly.
when_to_use: When injecting SEO meta tags for blog post or feed pages
When you need conditional Helmet wrapping based on environment
avoid: For non-meta UI content
When react-helmet is not available in the project

props.name: needHelmetWrapper
props.description: Whether to wrap content in react-helmet Helmet

examples:
<MetaWrapper
  needHelmetWrapper={true}
  metaComponent={
    <>
      <title>How we built our platform | Engineering Blog</title>
      <meta name="description" content="A deep dive into our platform architecture." />
      <meta property="og:title" content="How we built our platform" />
      <meta property="og:image" content="https://example.com/og-image.jpg" />
    </>
  }
/>

<MetaWrapper
  needHelmetWrapper={false}
  metaComponent={
    <>
      <meta name="description" content="Latest posts from the engineering team." />
    </>
  }
/>

name: Paginator
description: Renders a numbered page navigation bar for blog post listings with optional prev/next navigation buttons. Automatically hides when there is only one page and fires analytics events on page changes.
when_to_use: At the bottom of a paginated blog post listing
When post count exceeds itemsPerPage and pagination is needed
avoid: When using infinite scroll or load-more pattern — use the Posts component's showMore button instead
When totalItems < itemsPerPage (component renders nothing)

props.name: itemsPerPage
props.description: Number of posts displayed per page

props.name: onPageChange
props.description: Callback called with new page number

props.name: maxPages
props.description: Maximum number of pages to allow

props.name: queryParams
props.description: Current URL query params to preserve in page links

props.name: pageCountForShowSupportButtons
props.description: Minimum page count before prev/next buttons appear (default 6)

examples:
<Paginator
  itemsPerPage={10}
  totalItems={85}
  page={currentPage}
  queryParams={{ tag: 'frontend', service: 'cloud' }}
  pageCountForShowSupportButtons={4}
  onPageChange={handlePageChange}
/>

name: PostCard
description: Renders a single blog post preview card with image, title, description, tag, date, reading time, and like count. Supports multiple sizes and heading levels for proper document hierarchy.
when_to_use: Inside blog feed or listing pages to display a post summary
When rendering a grid of post previews
For suggested/related post sections
avoid: For full post display — use a post page layout instead
When post data lacks a URL — the card is not interactive without it

props.name: analyticsEvents
props.description: Analytics events to fire on card interaction

props.name: fullWidth
props.description: Whether card spans full container width

props.name: showTag
props.description: Whether to display the first post tag

examples:
<PostCard
  post={{
    id: 42,
    title: 'Building scalable microservices with Go',
    description: 'A practical guide to splitting monoliths and managing service mesh.',
    url: '/blog/scalable-microservices-go',
    image: '/images/posts/microservices.jpg',
    date: '2024-03-15',
    readingTime: 8,
    tags: [{ id: 'backend', name: 'Backend' }],
    likes: 24,
    hasUserLike: false,
  }}
  showTag
  size="s"
  titleHeadingLevel="h3"
/>

<PostCard
  post={pinnedPost}
  fullWidth
  showTag
  size="m"
  titleHeadingLevel="h2"
/>

name: PostInfo
description: Displays metadata for a blog post page: publication date, reading time, sharing options, and save/like button. Reads post context and supports custom extra info items via SettingsContext.
when_to_use: On a blog post detail page to show post metadata
When social sharing and save functionality is needed on a post
avoid: In feed card previews — use SuggestPostInfo or PostCard instead

props.name: theme
props.description: Color theme for the info bar

examples:
<PostInfo
  postId={123}
  date="2024-03-15"
  readingTime={7}
  theme="light"
/>

<PostInfo
  postId="how-we-built-our-platform"
  date="2024-01-20"
  readingTime={12}
  theme="dark"
  analyticsEventsContainer={{
    sharing: [{ name: 'blog_share_click', type: 'default-goal' }],
    save: [{ name: 'blog_save_click', type: 'default-goal' }],
  }}
  qa="post-info"
/>

name: Posts
description: Renders a full blog post listing with a grid of PostCard components, an optional pinned post, load-more button, error state, and pagination. Manages loading state visual feedback.
when_to_use: As the main content area of a blog feed page
When displaying a paginated or infinite-scroll list of blog posts
avoid: For individual post display
When only a few posts are shown statically — render PostCard directly

props.name: containerId
props.description: HTML id for the posts container element

props.name: isShowMoreVisible
props.description: Whether to show the load-more button

props.name: postCountOnPage
props.description: Total count of posts for pagination

props.name: pinnedPostOnPage
props.description: Post to pin at top (shown on page 1 only)

examples:
<Posts
  containerId="blog-posts"
  currentPage={1}
  postsOnPage={posts}
  isFetching={false}
  isShowMoreVisible={hasMore}
  errorShowMore={false}
  postCountOnPage={totalPosts}
  perPageInQuery={12}
  queryParams={{ tag: 'frontend' }}
  handleShowMore={async () => {
    await loadMorePosts();
  }}
  handlePageChange={async (page) => {
    await fetchPage(page);
  }}
/>

<Posts
  containerId="blog-posts-main"
  currentPage={page}
  postsOnPage={posts}
  pinnedPostOnPage={featuredPost}
  isFetching={isFetching}
  isShowMoreVisible={false}
  errorShowMore={hasError}
  postCountOnPage={totalCount}
  perPageInQuery={10}
  queryParams={queryParams}
  handleShowMore={handleShowMore}
  handlePageChange={handlePageChange}
/>

name: PostsEmpty
description: Renders an empty state message when no blog posts match the current filter or search criteria.
when_to_use: When the blog feed has no posts to display after filtering or searching
avoid: For error states — use PostsError instead
examples: {posts.length === 0 && !isFetching && <PostsEmpty />}

name: PostsError
description: Renders an error state for blog post loading failures with a retry button. Calls window.location.reload() if no custom handler is provided.
when_to_use: When blog post fetching fails and a user-facing error with retry is needed
avoid: For empty results — use PostsEmpty instead

props.name: onButtonClick
props.description: Custom retry handler; defaults to page reload

examples: {hasError && <PostsError />}

<PostsError
  onButtonClick={async () => {
    setError(false);
    await fetchPosts(queryParams);
  }}
/>

name: Prompt
description: A timed tooltip-style popup that displays a message with one or more action buttons. Automatically disappears after openDuration milliseconds and resets its timer when openTimestamp changes.
when_to_use: When prompting the user to take an action (e.g. sign in to like a post)
For transient, auto-dismissing notification messages with CTAs
avoid: For persistent dialogs or modals — use Modal from uikit instead
When no timed auto-dismiss behavior is needed

props.name: actions
props.description: Array of button props for action buttons

props.name: openTimestamp
props.description: Unix timestamp (ms) when prompt should open; resets timer on change

props.name: openDuration
props.description: Milliseconds to keep prompt visible before auto-close

props.name: theme
props.description: Visual theme for the prompt container

examples:
<Prompt
  text="Subscribe to get notified about new posts"
  openTimestamp={Date.now()}
  openDuration={6000}
  theme="grey"
  actions={[
    {
      children: 'Subscribe',
      view: 'action',
      size: 'l',
      onClick: handleSubscribe,
    },
    {
      children: 'Dismiss',
      view: 'flat',
      size: 'l',
      onClick: handleDismiss,
    },
  ]}
/>

name: PromptSignIn
description: A pre-configured Prompt that asks unauthenticated users to sign in, typically triggered when they attempt to like a post. Wraps Prompt with default sign-in text and action.
when_to_use: When an action requires authentication and you need to prompt the user to sign in
On blog post pages where liking requires a logged-in account
avoid: For general notifications — use Prompt with custom text instead

props.name: onClickSignIn
props.description: Handler called when sign-in button is clicked

examples:
<PromptSignIn
  text="You need to be signed in to save this post"
  openTimestamp={signInPromptTimestamp}
  onClickSignIn={handleSignInRedirect}
/>

name: Search
description: A debounced search input component for blog post filtering. Built on uikit TextInput with clear/submit icon and iPhone-safe autofocus behavior.
when_to_use: In blog FeedHeader to enable post filtering by keyword
When a debounced search-as-you-type input is needed
avoid: For form submission search — add a submit button separately
When search should not be debounced
examples:
<Search
  initialValue={queryParams.search ?? ''}
  onSubmit={(value) => {
    updateQueryParams({ search: value, page: 1 });
  }}
  placeholder="Search posts..."
  debounce={400}
/>

<Search
  initialValue=""
  value={searchValue}
  onSubmit={handleSearchChange}
  size="s"
  autoFocus
  debounce={300}
/>

name: Wrapper
description: A section wrapper component that applies configurable top, bottom, left, and right padding using BEM modifier classes. Used to frame blog page content blocks with consistent spacing.
when_to_use: To wrap blog content sections with consistent padding
When standardized vertical/horizontal spacing is required around a block
avoid: For non-blog UI sections — use layout components from uikit or page-constructor instead
examples:
<Wrapper>
  <Posts
    containerId="blog-posts"
    currentPage={1}
    postsOnPage={posts}
    isFetching={false}
    isShowMoreVisible={false}
    errorShowMore={false}
    postCountOnPage={30}
    perPageInQuery={10}
    queryParams={{}}
    handleShowMore={handleShowMore}
    handlePageChange={handlePageChange}
  />
</Wrapper>

<Wrapper
  paddings={{ top: 'l', bottom: 'xl', left: 'm', right: 'm' }}
  qa="blog-feed-section"
>
  <FeedHeader
    title="Engineering Blog"
    tags={tags}
    queryParams={queryParams}
    handleLoadData={handleLoadData}
  />
</Wrapper>
